title: Smoke test dispatcher lifecycle through Discord
description:
Goal: verify that the repaired multi-agent pipeline executes a small bounded task end-to-end. 

Scope:
- add one short note to docs/LIVE-SYSTEM-BEHAVIOR.md under the Discord Behavior section
- the note should say that important lifecycle events must appear in Discord, but Discord is not the source of truth
- commit the change with the normal agent/task commit prefix
- update canonical task state honestly
- if applicable, pass through test/review before completion

Success criteria:
- task is claimed and dispatched
- Discord shows important lifecycle updates
- dashboard status changes are real
- a branch/commit or PR artifact exists
- task ends in done or blocked honestly

Suggested task metadata:
- repo/project: rook-workspace
- status: Ready
- assigned agent: rook or leave it coordinator-owned if your board supports that
- priority: normal

commits.message: [agent:rook][task:ops-0018] add lifecycle events documentation to Discord Behavior section
commits.author: Marcus Grätsch
